name: Oma's Almond Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound Unblanched almonds, finely Ground or grated
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 30 minutes
Chilling time: 8 hours or overnight
Cooking time: 10 minutes
1. Cream butter and sugar in a large mixer bowl until light and fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time, then add lemon rind and juice. Stir in flour, baking powder, and salt. Mix in ground almonds to form a soft dough. Divide the dough into four portions.
2. Refrigerate each portion of dough wrapped in wax paper for at least 8 hours or overnight until firm.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease ungreased baking sheets.
4. Roll out one portion of chilled dough on a lightly floured pastry cloth using a rolling pin covered with a stocking, or between sheets of lightly floured wax paper, to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm). Cut into cookies and return the remaining dough to the refrigerator if it softens.
5. Place the cut cookies on prepared baking sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart. Sprinkle with colored sugar if desired.
6. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes until lightly browned at the edges. Remove from oven and transfer to wire racks to cool completely. Store in an airtight container.
This recipe honors Oma (grandmother) Antonia Drux, who immigrated from Germany in 1923. The cookies are thin, crisp, and delicate, perfect for holiday celebrations.
